title: The Floorboard In The Hall
central_metaphor: The single, unchanged floorboard that creaks underfoot represents the immutable, sensory memory of a childhood self, persisting beneath the surface changes of a now-alien home.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The gravel drive’s a little smoother now
The mailbox post is leaning south
I cut the engine, let the dust cloud settle down
And see the tire swing’s just one old rope
[Pre-Chorus]
The screen door’s got a different groan
A stranger’s welcome on the stone
[Chorus]
But the floorboard in the hall still knows my weight
The one that creaks a secret, low and slow
It sings the song of every running, late-for-dinner date
A truth that only me and this old house know
[Verse 2]
The kitchen smells of coffee, not of grease
And bacon frying in the pan
There’s modern art where momma’s cross-stitch used to be
A different life laid on the same old land
[Pre-Chorus]
The screen door’s got a different groan
A stranger’s welcome on the stone
[Chorus]
But the floorboard in the hall still knows my weight
The one that creaks a secret, low and slow
It sings the song of every running, late-for-dinner date
A truth that only me and this old house know
[Bridge]
I guess new wood forgets the years
And fresh paint covers up the fears
But deep down in the bones, beneath the change
Some memories are built too strong to rearrange
[Final Chorus]
But the floorboard in the hall still knows my weight
The one that creaks a secret, low and slow
It sings the song of every running, late-for-dinner date
A truth that only this old house and ghosts know
